spec_match.rationale: Captures the headline that v134 clears cache (not just cookies/storage) for bounce-tracker-flagged origins, but the heuristic shown (shortStay = ms < 10000) is wildly different from the spec's threshold (the PrivacyCG draft uses '<= 10s without user interaction' but Chrome internally uses '<=10 second dwell time + no UI'). Dwell-as-only-input misses the no-interaction predicate.

interactive.rationale: Range slider for dwell time + v134 toggle + a 'run flow' button that re-renders the 5-step visualiser and a verdict block. Three real interactive surfaces.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises a simulator of the bounce flow; page delivers the dwell + toggle + verdict triplet with the v134 cache-clear consequence spelled out.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles cleared vs preserved verdicts, but doesn't show the 'tracker had user interaction' exception path (which is the entire reason SSO works), and doesn't show the grace-period delay before mitigation fires.

references.rationale: see-also links chromestatus.com/feature/6299570819301376 and the PrivacyCG nav-tracking mitigations explainer.

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, .verdict.preserved uses --accent-rose border + matching foreground rather than coloured background; step.tracker has accent-rose background but inherits text color which needs WCAG verification.

openQuestions.title: Add the user-interaction exception
openQuestions.detail: The spec's flagging predicate is 'short stay AND no user interaction during the bounce'. The simulator only checks dwell time, so a 5-second pause with active typing would falsely get flagged. Add a 'user interacted with tracker' checkbox and require both conditions for the flag, so the SSO / OAuth carve-out is visible.

openQuestions.title: Verify step.tracker contrast
openQuestions.detail: .step.tracker sets background: var(--accent-rose) but no foreground colour, so text inherits --text-charcoal on accent-rose. AA at 0.78rem is 4.5:1 — check this specific pair and either set color: var(--bg-ivory) or pick a lighter background variant.

openQuestions.title: Use a real-ish dwell threshold
openQuestions.detail: Hard-coded `ms < 10_000` is OK as a heuristic but the page doesn't explain where 10s comes from. Either link to the PrivacyCG draft section that defines the dwell window, or annotate the threshold inline.

openQuestions.title: Show the grace-period delay
openQuestions.detail: Mitigation is applied after a grace period (currently ~1h on stable), not synchronously. Add a 'time until purge' note to the verdict so visitors understand the action is deferred, not immediate.

summary: Solid five-step flow with a clean verdict and the right v134 framing, but the heuristic is too thin (no interaction predicate) and the contrast on step.tracker needs a check. Quick wins.
